full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Relationship with God is not a quid pro quo, a favor for a favor.<|9.70|> <|10.20|> And yet, that assumption of this for that with God thrives and flourishes age after age.<|21.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It is said that in ancient Israel and Judah, communal fasts were instituted in times of<|6.84|> <|6.84|> crisis during a famine or plague.<|10.16|> <|11.34|> One scholar says that the underlying idea of such fasts was that such practices would<|18.00|> <|18.00|> engage the compassion of the deity and the disaster would end.<|24.16|> <|24.16|> Amen.<|24.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If we fast and pray and mourn and wear sackcloth and throw ashes on our heads, the reasoning goes.<|8.74|> <|10.06|> God will certainly end the crisis.<|12.28|> <|13.92|> If I do this, God will do that.<|17.72|> <|19.28|> This is what one can call rabbit's foot theology.<|23.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.00|> That was supposed to be really funny.<|3.18|> <|5.20|> And it did not land well at 8 o'clock, and it did not land well at 1030,<|9.98|> <|10.20|> so I will not say that again.<|12.78|> <|16.08|> Richard laughed.<|17.30|> <|17.94|> But Richard cannot be trusted when it comes to things that are funny or not.<|23.54|> <|24.38|> Okay.<|24.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Why did we fast and you did not see?<|4.04|> <|4.68|> We afflicted ourselves and you took no note.<|8.78|> <|10.10|> God here is being interrogated by sincere people who wonder why their liturgical antics,<|18.50|> <|18.94|> their ecclesial gimmicks, aren't bringing an end to their suffering.<|24.16|> <|24.16|> Amen.<|24.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And God responds. But before we reflect on God's response, let's press pause for some context.<|9.64|> <|10.94|> The God before which these fussy worshipers fast is not just any God.<|16.76|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This is the God of their ancestors,<|3.96|> <|4.42|> the God who sent the prophet Moses before Pharaoh,<|7.34|> <|7.34|> the most powerful man in the world,<|9.50|> <|9.70|> and said, let my people go.<|11.70|> <|12.28|> The God who not only extracted them<|14.68|> <|14.68|> from Egypt's economy of exploitation,<|17.24|> <|17.64|> but tasked them to never repeat<|20.56|> <|20.56|> economics of enslavement ever again.<|23.26|> <|23.26|> Amen.<|23.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Remember that you were enslaved in Egypt, and I redeemed you from there.<|6.00|> <|7.18|> Remember to never do to others what was done to you by the empire, by the powerful,<|12.96|> <|12.96|> by those who do not have your best interest in mind.<|16.06|> <|16.46|> Do not replicate their death-dealing ways of relating.<|21.08|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.56|> Now we can press play.<|2.74|> <|4.16|> Why did we fast and you did not see?<|7.30|> <|7.30|> Afflicted ourselves and you took no note.<|10.88|> <|10.88|> Look, says the God of Israel.<|13.54|> <|13.54|> You fast and cover yourself with ashes and sackcloth<|18.18|> <|18.18|> and yet you continue to oppress workers.<|22.18|> <|23.52|> This is God's exasperation.<|25.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> that the people of God are more concerned with liturgical self-help routines<|6.38|> <|6.38|> than they are with enacting for others the justice God enacted for them oh so long ago.<|13.52|> <|14.62|> God is exasperated quo<|7.90|> <|7.90|> is an appropriate way to get God's attention as if God in some way is no longer paying attention to us.<|15.98|> <|16.90|> And we will again hear the poet's words ringing in our There is nothing we can do to impress God. There is nothing we can do to impress God or garner God's favor.<|17.70|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> God made us as we are and is thus already impressed by God's own work, if you will.<|9.32|> <|10.80|> God is already impressed with God's own work.<|13.88|> <|14.22|> You are God's work.<|15.28|> <|15.66|> God's love for us and claim on our lives is settled and indissoluble.<|21.96|> <|22.42|> Full stop.<|23.30|> <|23.30|> Full stop.<|24.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And the love of God we relish, the love of God we celebrate, must never be hoarded.<|9.88|> <|10.92|> Never hoarded.<|12.26|> <|14.16|> Instead, it is our joy to share it with more and more.<|21.72|> <|23.08|> All the more.<|24.44|> <|26.44|> Amen.<|26.96|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|2.00|><|endoftext|>
